channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Cloudflare targets 2029 for full post-quantum security
item.description: Recent advances in quantum hardware and software have accelerated the timeline on which quantum attack might happen. Cloudflare is responding by moving our target for full post-quantum security to 2029.

item.title: Authorities disrupt router DNS hijacks used to steal Microsoft 365 logins
item.description: An international operation from law enforcement authorities in partnership with private companies has disrupted FrostArmada, an APT28 campaign hijacking local traffic from MikroTik and TP-Link routers to steal Microso...

item.title: Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs
item.description: The improper validation of user-supplied JavaScript code allows attackers to execute arbitrary code and access the file system. The post Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs appeared first on Security...

item.title: Russian APT28 Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Credentials, UK Security Agency Warns
item.description: Newly identified malicious campaigns are linked to virtual private servers modified by APT28 to operate as malicious DNS servers

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.description: A high-severity security vulnerability has been disclosed in Docker Engine that could permit an attacker to bypass authorization plugins (AuthZ) under specific circumstances. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-340...

item.title: GPU Rowhammer Attack Enables Privilege Escalation and Full System Compromise
item.description: GPUBreach uses GPU Rowhammer on GDDR6 to flip bits, corrupt page tables and escalate to system root

item.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
item.description: Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference.

item.title: Human vs AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
item.description: As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making.

item.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
item.description: A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results.

item.title: Severe StrongBox Vulnerability Patched in Android
item.description: A critical DoS vulnerability in the Framework component of Android has also been fixed with the latest update. The post Severe StrongBox Vulnerability Patched in Android appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Why Your Automated Pentesting Tool Just Hit a Wall
item.description: Automated pentesting tools deliver strong early results, then quickly plateau. Picus Security explains how the "PoC cliff" leaves major attack surfaces untested and creates a dangerous validation gap. [...]

item.title: GrafanaGhost Exploit Bypasses AI Guardrails for Silent Data Exfiltration
item.description: GrafanaGhost chains AI prompt injection and URL flaws to exfiltrate sensitive Grafana data

item.title: GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data
item.description: By targeting Grafana’s AI components, attackers can point to external resources and inject indirect prompts to bypass safeguards. The post GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data appeared fir...

item.title: Major outage cripples Russian banking apps and metro payments nationwide
item.description: A major outage hit Russian banking apps and payments, blocking card use, cash withdrawals, and mobile access for hours. A widespread outage disrupted banking apps and payment systems across Russia, leaving customers u...

item.title: Acronis MDR by TRU brings 24/7 managed detection and response to MSPs
item.description: Acronis has announced the launch of Acronis MDR by Acronis TRU, a globally available 24/7/365 managed detection and response (MDR) service. Built specifically for managed service providers (MSPs) of all sizes, the ser...

item.title: Fast-moving Storm-1175 uses new exploits to breach networks and drop Medusa
item.description: China-based actor Storm-1175 runs fast ransomware attacks, exploiting new flaws to breach systems and quickly deploy Medusa ransomware. China-based actor Storm-1175 carries out fast, financially driven ransomware atta...

item.title: Webinar Today: Why Automated Pentesting Alone Is Not Enough
item.description: Join the live diagnostic session to expose hidden coverage gaps and shift from flawed tool-level evaluations to a comprehensive, program-level validation discipline. The post Webinar Today: Why Automated Pentesting Al...

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. "A purpose-built Python sca...

item.title: The Trojan horse of cybercrime: Weaponizing SaaS notification pipelines
item.description: Cisco Talos has recently observed an increase in activity that is leveraging notification pipelines in popular collaboration platforms to deliver spam and phishing emails.

item.title: Hong Kong Police Can Force You to Reveal Your Encryption Keys
item.description: According to a new law, the Hong Kong police can demand that you reveal the encryption keys protecting your computer, phone, hard drives, etc.—even if you are just transiting the airport. In a security alert dated Mar...

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI gets a second-opinion feature built on cross-model review
item.description: Coding agents make decisions in sequence: a plan is drafted, implemented, then tested. Any error introduced early compounds as subsequent steps build on the same flawed assumption. Self-reflection is a recognized miti...

item.title: Fortinet Releases Emergency Patch After FortiClient EMS Bug Is Exploited
item.description: Fortinet has updated its FortiClient EMS product after zero-day attacks surfaced

item.title: German Police Unmask REvil Ransomware Leader
item.description: Shchukin is accused of extorting more than $2 million as the head of the GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations. The post German Police Unmask REvil Ransomware Leader appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Fortinet FortiClient EMS to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Fortinet FortiClient EMS to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)...

item.title: Comp AI: The open-source way to get compliant with SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA and GDPR
item.description: Getting a startup through a SOC 2 audit has long meant months of manual evidence collection, policy writing, and repeated back-and-forth with auditors. A growing number of compliance platforms have moved to automate p...

item.title: OpenAI opens applications for an external AI safety research fellowship
item.description: OpenAI is accepting applications for a paid fellowship program that will fund external researchers to work on safety and alignment questions related to advanced AI systems. The program, called the OpenAI Safety Fellow...

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take full control of a...

item.title: White House Seeks to Slash CISA Funding by $707 Million
item.description: The Trump administration says the FY2027 budget refocuses CISA on its core mission: protecting federal agencies and critical infrastructure. The post White House Seeks to Slash CISA Funding by $707 Million appeared fi...

item.title: Experts published unpatched Windows zero-day BlueHammer
item.description: A researcher leaked the unpatched Windows zero-day “BlueHammer,” letting attackers gain SYSTEM rights; no patch exists yet. A disgruntled researcher released the BlueHammer Windows zero-day, a privilege escalation fla...

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and break into suscep...

item.title: The case for fixing CWE weakness patterns instead of patching one bug at a time
item.description: In this Help Net Security interview, Alec Summers, MITRE CVE/CWE Project Lead, discusses how CWE is moving from a background reference into active use in vulnerability disclosure. More CVE records now include CWE mapp...

item.title: How Mimecast brings enterprise-grade email protection to API deployment
item.description: In this Help Net Security video, Andrew Williams, Senior Product Manager at Mimecast, walks through the company’s API-based email security protection for Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace environments. The video cove...

item.title: Google study finds LLMs are embedded at every stage of abuse detection
item.description: Online platforms are running large language models at every stage of LLM content moderation, from generating training data to auditing their own systems for bias. Researchers at Google mapped how this is happening acr...

item.title: Axios Attack Shows Social Complex Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.

item.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.

item.title: Phishing LNK files and GitHub C2 power new DPRK cyber attacks
item.description: DPRK-linked hackers use GitHub C2s, starting attacks via phishing LNK files that drop a PDF and PowerShell script in South Korea. North Korea-linked threat actors target South Korean organizations using GitHub as C2 s...

item.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
item.description: Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]

item.title: New Mexico’s Meta Ruling and Encryption
item.description: Mike Masnick points out that the recent New Mexico court ruling against Meta has some bad implications for end-to-end encryption, and security in general: If the “design choices create liability” framework seems worry...

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The activity, assessed to be on...

item.title: Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents
item.description: A vulnerability named ‘AI Agent Traps’ allows attackers to manipulate, deceive, and exploit visiting agents via malicious web content. The post Google DeepMind Researchers Map Web Attacks Against AI Agents appeared fi...

item.title: BKA unmasks two REvil Ransomware operators behind 130+ German attacks
item.description: German police BKA identified two key REvil ransomware members, linking them to over 130 attacks in Germany. Germany’s Federal Criminal Police (BKA) has identified two key figures behind the REvil ransomware group, lin...

item.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
item.description: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.

item.title: Why Simple Breach Monitoring is No Longer Enough
item.description: Infostealers are harvesting credentials and session cookies at scale, bypassing traditional defenses. Lunar explains why simple breach monitoring alone can't keep up with modern credential-based attacks. [...]

item.title: AI Is Entering Your Infrastructure. Now what?
item.description: The Tool Becomes the Operator AI is quietly moving from the chat window into the infrastructure itself. It is no longer just answering questions. It is writing code, interacting with APIs, responding to customers, and...

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards groups recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.

item.title: Attackers Exploit RCE Flaw as 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM Instances Remain Exposed
item.description: Over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances remain exposed online, as attackers actively exploit a critical remote code execution flaw CVE-2025-53521. Over 14,000 F5 BIG-IP APM instances remain exposed online, with attackers...

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no longer lives on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, an...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.description: This week had real hits. The key software got tampered with. Active bugs showed up in the tools people use every day. Some attacks didn’t even need much effort because the path was already there. One weak spot now spr...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-35616 - Fortinet FortiClient EMS Improper Access Control Vulnerability Thi...

item.title: How often are redirects used in phishing in 2026?, (Mon, Apr 6th)
item.description: In one of his recent diaries, Johannes discussed how open redirects are actively being sought out by threat actors[1], which made me wonder about how commonly these mechanisms are actually misusedâ€¦

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin head...

item.title: Security briefing: March 2026
Traffic violation scams switch to QR codes in new phishing texts
item.description: Scammers are sending fake "Notice of Default" traffic violation text messages impersonating state courts across the U.S., pressuring recipients to scan a QR code that leads to a phishing site demanding a $6.99 payment...

item.title: New FortiClient EMS flaw exploited in attacks, emergency patch released
item.description: Fortinet has released an emergency weekend security update for a new critical FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) vulnerability that is actively exploited in attacks. [...]

item.title: $285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
item.description: Drift has revealed that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to the theft of $285 million was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic...

item.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 91
item.description: Security Affairs Malware newsletter includes a collection of the best articles and research on malware in the international landscape Infiniti Stealer: a new macOS infostealer using ClickFix and Python/Nuitka Convergi...

item.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
item.description: Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]

item.title: Axios npm hack used fake Teams error fix to hijack maintainer account
item.description: The maintainers of the popular Axios HTTP client have published a detailed post-mortem describing how one of its developers was targeted by a social engineering campaign believed to have been conducted by North Korean...

item.title: Elastic Security Integrations Roundup: Q1 2026
item.description: Elastic Security Labs announces nine new integrations for Elastic Security spanning cloud security, endpoint visibility, email threat detection, identity and SIEM.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.description: Here’s a fossil of a 150-million year old fish that choked to death on a belemnite rostrum : the hard, internal shell of an extinct, squid-like animal. Original paper . As usual, you can also use this squid post to ta...

item.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
item.description: "Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs" can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research.

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 006 - CERT-EU Confirms European Commission Cloud Breach, Sportradar Details Emerge, and Mandiant Quantifies Campaign at 1,000+ SaaS Environments, (Fri, Apr 3rd)
item.description: This is the sixth update to the TeamPCP supply chain campaign threat intelligence report,&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;"When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon"&#;x26;#;xc2;&#;x26;#;xa0;(v3.0, March 25, 2026).&#;x26;#...

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.

item.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
item.description: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open source artifacts across containers, libraries, agent skills, and GitHub Actions.

item.title: Orca Security Featured in SACR’s 2026 Unified Agentic Defense Platforms Report
item.description: Software Analyst Cyber Research (SACR) has published its report, The Convergence of AI and Data Security: An Industry-Wide Technoscope of Unified Agentic Defense Platforms. In this evaluation of 15 leading vendors sha...

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new version of the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile operating system...

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. "Earlier today, a malicious actor gained...

item.title: New Phishing Platform Used in Credential Theft Campaigns Against C-Suite Execs
item.description: A large-scale credential theft campaign targeting senior executives has been linked to a previously unknown automated phishing platform called Venom

item.title: Axios npm Supply Chain Compromise
item.description: What is the Attack? A software supply chain attack targeted the widely used JavaScript library Axios after an attacker reportedly compromised a maintainer’s npm account and published malicious package versions 1.14.1...

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.

item.title: Four security principles for agentic AI systems
item.description: Agentic AI represents a qualitative shift in how software operates. Traditional software executes deterministic instructions. Generative AI responds to human prompts with output that humans review and use at their dis...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secret...

item.title: Security Bosses Are All-In on AI. Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future promise.

item.title: New 'Storm' Infostealer Remotely Decrypts Stolen Credentials
item.description: This modern infostealer adopted server-side decryption of stolen credentials to bypass security controls

item.title: Attempts to Exploit Exposed "Vite" Installs (CVE-2025-30208), (Thu, Apr 2nd)
item.description: From its GitHub repo: "Vite (French word for "quick", pronounced /vi?t/, like "veet") is a new breed of frontend build tooling that significantly improves the frontend development experience" [https://github.com/vitej...

item.title: NCSC Issues Security Alert Over Hackers Targeting WhatsApp and Signal Accounts
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency offered advice to “high-risk’ individuals” on how to protect against social engineering and cyber-attacks

item.title: vSphere and BRICKSTORM Malware: A Defender's Guide
item.description: Written by: Stuart Carrera Introduction Building on recent BRICKSTORM research from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), this post explores the evolving threats facing virtualized environments. These operations di...

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18 Security Updates Amid DarkSword Threat
item.description: iOS/iPadOS 18.7.7 updates expanded to protect older devices from DarkSword web exploit kit

item.title: [Video] The TTP Ep 21: When Attackers Become Trusted Users
item.description: An episode of the Talos Threat Perspective on the 2025 Year in Review trends. We explore how identity is being used to gain, extend, and maintain access inside environments.

item.title: Why we're rethinking cache for the AI era
item.description: The explosion of AI-bot traffic, representing over 10 billion requests per week, has opened up new challenges and opportunities for cache design. We look at some of the ways AI bot traffic differs from humans, how thi...

item.title: Researchers Observe Sub-One-Hour Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Halcyon says Akira is now capable of carrying out an entire ransomware attack in less than an hour

item.title: GitHub Used as Covert Channel in Multi-Stage Malware Campaign
item.description: LNK files use GitHub C2, embedded decoders and PowerShell for persistence and data exfiltration

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy reality of keeping sys...

item.title: UAT-10608: Inside a large-scale automated credential harvesting operation targeting web applications
item.description: Talos is disclosing a large-scale automated credential harvesting campaign carried out by a threat cluster we currently track as UAT-10608. The campaign is primarily leveraging a collection framework dubbed “NEXUS Lis...

item.title: Qilin EDR killer infection chain
item.description: This blog provides an in-depth analysis of the malicious “msimg32.dll” used in Qilin ransomware attacks, which is a multi-stage infection chain targeting EDR systems.

item.title: Inside the Talos 2025 Year in Review: A discussion on what the data means for defenders
item.description: A conversation between Cisco Talos and Cisco Security leaders on the 2025 threat landscape, from identity attacks and legacy vulnerabilities to AI-driven threats, and what defenders should prioritize now.

item.title: An overview of ransomware threats in Japan in 2025 and early detection insights from Qilin cases
item.description: There were 134 ransomware incidents reported in Japan in 2025, representing a 17.5% year-over-year increase from 2024.

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7 and iPadOS 18.7.7 to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known as DarkSword. "We enabled the a...

item.title: The Sysdig MCP server is now available in AWS Marketplace
item.description: Correlating security signals from cloud services, container registries, and runtime environments is an important component of maintaining secure cloud deployments. But making this a seamless process, without the need...

item.title: Risk isn’t reduced until you take action: How teams resolve issues in the cloud
item.description: Effective remediation depends on real-time insight that allows security teams to quickly investigate, understand, and respond to threats with precision. By taking targeted actions like stopping compromised workloads,...

item.title: Malicious Script That Gets Rid of ADS, (Wed, Apr 1st)
item.description: Today, most malware are called â€œfilelessâ€ because they try to reduce their footprint on the infected computer filesystem to the bare minimum. But they need to write somethingâ€¦ think about persistence. They can us...

item.title: Google Introduces Android Dev Verification Amid Openness Debate
item.description: Android requires dev identity verification for sideloaded apps; phased global rollout from September

item.title: Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Commoditizes ClickFix Attacks
item.description: A new service on the cybercrime market provides automated capabilities to create persistent information-stealing social engineering attacks.

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026, leverages these scripts to...

item.title: New Venom Stealer MaaS Platform Automates Continuous Data Theft
item.description: Venom Stealer malware-as-a-service automates ClickFix social engineering, credential and crypto exfiltration

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 005 - First Confirmed Victim Disclosure, Post-Compromise Cloud Enumeration Documented, and Axios Attribution Narrows, (Wed, Apr 1st)
item.description: This is the fifth update to the TeamPCP supply chain campaign threat intelligence report, "When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon" (v3.0, March 25, 2026). Update 004 covered developments through March 30, includi...

item.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
item.description: There is a character that keeps appearing in enterprise security departments, and most CISOs know exactly who that is. It doesn’t build. It doesn’t enable. Its entire function is to say "No." No to ChatGPT. No to Deep...

item.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
item.description: A multi-pronged phishing campaign is targeting Spanish-speaking users in organizations across Latin America and Europe to deliver Windows banking trojans like Casbaneiro (aka Metamorfo) via another malware called Hora...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-5281 Google Dawn Use-After-Free Vulnerability This type of vulnerability i...

item.title: New Chrome Zero-Day CVE-2026-5281 Under Active Exploitation — Patch Released
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address 21 vulnerabilities, including a zero-day flaw that it said has been exploited in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-5...

item.title: A Taxonomy of Cognitive Security
item.description: Last week, I listened to a fascinating talk by K. Melton on cognitive security, cognitive hacking, and reality pentesting. The slides from the talk are here , but—even better—Menton has a long essay laying out the bas...

item.title: Eight in 10 UK Manufacturers Hit by Cyber Incident in a Year
item.description: Most UK manufacturers compromised last year suffered financial loss, says ESET

item.title: Hackers Hijack Axios npm Package to Spread RATs
item.description: Threat actors hijacked the popular npm package axios to spread RAT malware after compromising an open‑source maintainer’s account, researchers warn

item.title: Google Attributes Axios npm Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Group UNC1069
item.description: Google has formally attributed the supply chain compromise of the popular Axios npm package to a financially motivated North Korean threat activity cluster tracked as UNC1069. "We have attributed the attack to a suspe...

item.title: Elastic releases detections for the Axios supply chain compromise
item.description: Hunting and detection rules for the Elastic-discovered Axios supply chain compromise.

item.title: Weaponizing the Protectors: TeamPCP’s Multi-Stage Supply Chain Attack on Security Infrastructure
item.description: TeamPCP continues its string of supply chain attacks, and announces a partnership with Vect ransomware group. The post Weaponizing the Protectors: TeamPCP’s Multi-Stage Supply Chain Attack on Security Infrastructure a...

item.title: Axios NPM Package Compromised in Precision Attack
item.description: The NPM package for Axios, a popular JavaScript HTTP client library, was briefly compromised this week, possibly by North Korean threat actors.

item.title: New compliance guide available: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 on AWS
item.description: We’re excited to announce the release of our latest compliance guide, ISO/IEC 27001:2022 on AWS, which provides practical guidance for organizations designing and operating an Information Security Management System (I...

item.title: Google's Vertex AI Is Over-Privileged. That's a Problem
item.description: Palo Alto Networks researchers show how attackers could exploit AI agents on Google's Vertex AI to steal data and break into restricted cloud infrastructure.

item.title: TeamPCP Breaches Cloud, SaaS Instances With Stolen Credentials
item.description: The threat group's shift to speedy attacks on AWS, Azure, and SaaS instances shows organizations need to respond quickly to compromised credentials.

item.title: Maryland Man Charged Over $53m Uranium Finance Crypto Hack
item.description: Maryland man accused of $53m Uranium Finance hack, exploited smart contract flaws, laundered funds

item.title: AI and Quantum Are Forcing a Rethink of Digital Trust
item.description: In a conversation with Dark Reading’s Terry Sweeney, DigiCert CEO Amit Sinha explains how AI-driven identities and quantum threats are reshaping the foundations of digital trust.

item.title: Phantom Project Bundles Infostealer, Crypter and RAT For Sale
item.description: Phantom Stealer .NET harvests browser credentials, cookies, cards, sessions, as stealer-as-a-service

item.title: Vertex AI Vulnerability Exposes Google Cloud Data and Private Artifacts
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security "blind spot" in Google Cloud's Vertex AI platform that could allow artificial intelligence (AI) agents to be weaponized by an attacker to gain unauthorized access to...

item.title: ChatGPT Security Issue Enabled Data Theft via Single Prompt
item.description: OpenAI has patched vulnerability, which Check Point said was because of a DNS loophole

item.title: Ransomware in 2025: Blending in is the strategy
item.description: A summary of the top ransomware trends from the Talos 2025 Year in Review, with a focus on identity, attacker tactics, and practical defenses.

item.title: NCSC Urges Immediate Patching of F5 BIG-IP Bug
item.description: The National Cyber Security Centre wants UK firms to patch CVE-2025-53521

item.title: Axios NPM Distribution Compromised in Supply Chain Attack
item.description: A compromised axios maintainer account led to malicious npm releases that propagated across environments. Learn how to assess impact, detect compromise, and secure your development workflows.

item.title: Fake Installers to Monero: A Multi-Tool Mining Operation
item.description: Elastic Security Labs dissects a long-running operation deploying RATs, cryptominers, and CPA fraud through fake installer lures, tracking its evolution across campaigns and Monero payouts.

item.title: Tracking TeamPCP: Investigating Post-Compromise Attacks Seen in the Wild
item.description: How TeamPCP are leveraging stolen secrets from the recent supply chain attacks to compromise cloud environments
